The Science (And Art) Of A Stronger Body & Mind
Thousands of clinical studies support creatine’s vast benefits for body and mind. While your body makes a small amount, it’s far less than it can store with supplementation. This is why creatine has been a very popular supplement for over 30 years, fueling athletes, entrepreneurs, and aging adults. Qualia Creatine+ was created to optimize these benefits with scientific precision by combining two clinically studied forms—creatine monohydrate (as micronized OptiCreatine™) and Creatine MagnaPower® (with magnesium to activate creatine into phosphocreatine, the fuel your body uses to make ATP) —plus sea salt for absorption. This helps overcome common issues with creatine monohydrate, including poor dissolution, gritty texture, and sedimentation.\
Creatine MagnaPower® (Magnesium Creatine Chelate)
Creatine converts to phosphocreatine in brain and muscle cells, using ATP bound to magnesium. FAQs
What does creatine do?
+
Creatine is a naturally occurring compound found in your muscles. Creatine plays a key role in energy production, especially in tissues that have high and fluctuating energy demands such as muscle and brain, which is why the majority of studies on creatine are on exercise and cognition.  
Qualia Creatine+ combines three ingredients—OptiCreatine™ Creatine Monohydrate, Creatine MagnaPower® (a magnesium creatine chelate), and sea salt. The two creatine forms help quickly replenish ATP, the body’s universal energy currency. Magnesium supports this ATP process, while sea salt supports creatine transport into cells in the brain, muscles, and intestines.\
What benefits should I expect when taking Qualia Creatine+?
+
The best-known benefits of creatine supplements like Qualia Creatine+ include enhanced muscle strength, size, and recovery, along with better performance during high-intensity exercise. Qualia Creatine+ also nourishes brain energy and function—some people may notice support for focus, memory, mental clarity, sustained energy, and overall mood.\
What makes Qualia Creatine+ different from other creatine supplements?
+
Qualia Creatine+ was designed to take creatine to the next level by combining scientific precision with premium ingredients. It features two clinically studied forms—creatine monohydrate (as micronized OptiCreatine™) and Creatine MagnaPower®, which binds creatine to magnesium to help convert it into phosphocreatine, the fuel your body uses to make ATP (cellular energy).  
To enhance absorption and performance, Qualia Creatine+ also includes sea salt, and its smooth, water-soluble powder delivers an easy, enjoyable experience. Together, these elements set a new standard in creatine supplementation, supporting muscle performance, brain energy, and healthy aging.\
Does creatine cause bloating or water retention?
+
Some people notice temporary bloating or "fullness" when they start creatine, but it’s usually not from water under the skin. Research shows that creatine draws water into muscle cells, increasing intracellular hydration — that’s what can make muscles feel fuller or slightly heavier. That shift supports cell hydration and is actually beneficial for muscle function, performance, and recovery.\  
If you do feel bloated, it’s often digestive, especially with larger doses or taking it on an empty stomach. Try lowering the dose, or splitting it across meals, to find your own comfort threshold.
Can I take Qualia Creatine+ with other Qualia products like Qualia Mind or Qualia NAD⁺?
+
Yes — Qualia Creatine+ is designed to complement other Qualia formulations to support whole-body energy, focus, and performance. Each formula targets different aspects of cellular and cognitive function, so when used together, they work synergistically to help you feel and perform at your best.\
How does creatine support brain and muscle performance?
+
Creatine helps your body and brain make energy more efficiently by converting to phosphocreatine—the fuel used to regenerate ATP, your cells’ primary energy source. Thousands of studies show creatine supports strength, endurance, and cognitive performance. While your body naturally makes some creatine, supplementation allows cells to store much more for high-demand energy needs. Qualia Creatine+ enhances these benefits with two clinically studied forms: micronized OptiCreatine™ (creatine monohydrate) and Creatine MagnaPower® (creatine magnesium chelate). Together, they optimize phosphocreatine levels in both brain and muscle to power performance, focus, and recovery.\
How many grams of creatine is in one scoop of Qualia Creatine+?
+
Each serving provides 5 grams of creatine from two premium sources—OptiCreatine™ (creatine monohydrate) and Creatine MagnaPower® (magnesium creatine chelate)—to help fuel active brain and muscle cells. A touch of sea salt is included to further support creatine absorption and transport into cells.\
Can I take creatine daily?
+
Yes, for best results, take 1 scoop of Qualia Creatine+ daily. This amount provides roughly the same dose of creatine monohydrate used in many research studies. Because creatine needs can vary by body weight, some people may prefer a smaller amount—around ½ scoop—as a general maintenance dose for average weight. You can adjust your serving to fit your goals and needs.\
What is creatine loading?
+
Some people use a creatine loading phase to raise muscle creatine levels more quickly. This typically lasts 5–7 days, with the equivalent of about 4 scoops of Qualia Creatine+ per day, divided into smaller servings. After loading, a maintenance phase follows with a lower daily amount—around ½ to 1 scoop (3–5 grams)—to sustain creatine levels. However, we don’t recommend creatine loading, as it may cause digestive discomfort for some individuals. Similar increases in tissue creatine can be achieved simply by taking Qualia Creatine+ consistently each day.\
Do I need to cycle Qualia Creatine+ or take breaks?
+
No cycling is required. Qualia Creatine+ can be taken daily as part of a consistent routine. If you choose to take breaks, it’s optional and based on personal preference rather than safety concerns.

What Qualia Focus Supports:
When your brain is thriving, you feel an effortless balance between mental energy and calm poise, with focused motivation and lasting clarity. Qualia Focus supports these key variables for healthy cognition in balance with each other. \
 Focus & Concentration\

Focus On What Matters.

Brain health is at the core of well-being.
But nutritionally supporting brain health naturally (and well), takes expert formulation. Our science team has researched hundreds of potential brain health ingredients for nearly a decade. Qualia Focus carefully crafts 14 of the most vital and indispensable ingredients on earth to support optimal cognitive health.
 
The Science (And Art) Of Naturally Supporting Brain Health
Balance matters. Ideal nourishment for brain function tries to support, rather than override, our brain’s regulatory pathways. We formulated Qualia Focus to support the natural regulatory capacities of the human brain, from dopamine and glutamate signaling, to blood flow, stress resilience, and synaptic plasticity. Qualia Focus is the result of thousands of hours of research to help harmonize these aspects of cognitive health for a thriving mindset that lasts. And formulating it required diverse considerations, such as:

Ancient Wisdom
We explored little known and long forgotten natural ingredients used in ancient medicinal health practices to promote mindset and brain health, such as Celastrus paniculatus, known for centuries in South Asia as “the intellect tree”, for its powerful capacity to support cognitive health.

Impeccable Ingredient Sourcing
All ingredients in Qualia Focus are vegan, non-GMO, and gluten free. The formula also includes potent and premium plant extracts from species such as ginger root, Ginkgo biloba, and American Ginseng, to achieve its powerful enhancements to mood and mental performance.

Relationships Between Ingredients
Our science team pioneered the use of complex systems science in supplement formulations, which values the sum total effect of different ingredients in combination with each other, including deep study of their ideal dosage relationships, to achieve the greatest health benefits.
Qualia Focus Ingredients

Celastrus paniculatus Seed Extract
Celastrus paniculatus is also known in South Asia as “the intellect tree” and has been used for centuries as a brain tonic to support mental sharpness and memory. It supports healthy acetylcholine signaling, which is an important neurotransmitter involved in attention, learning, memory, and neuroplasticity.

American Ginseng Root Extract
American ginseng (Panax quinquefolius) is a variety of ginseng native to forested regions in North America. It is the same genus as Asian ginseng (Panax ginseng) and prized for many of the same reasons—invigorating and fortifying in times of higher stress or greater demands. It contains similar active constituents called ginsenosides. Ginsenosides are thought to be responsible for many of the adaptogenic (i.e., stress and fatigue support) and  properties associated with ginseng.

Gingko biloba Leaf Extract
Gingko biloba is one of the most researched and widely used herbs for brain health. Gingko helps to uphold several neurotransmitter systems, neuroplasticity, healthy cerebral blood flow. It also helps support attention, concentration, and working memory.

Ginger Root Extract
Ginger is the common name for the whole or cut rhizome—the underground stem or vertical portion of the root—of the plant Zingiber officinale. Ginger has a long history of use as a food spice and had a long history for healing properties in Ayurveda and other healing systems. Modern science has discovered that ginger is a bioenhancer, positively influencing the bioavailability of many other compounds, with additional research towards its uses in other areas of health, including brain, gastrointestinal, metabolic, musculoskeletal, and vision.\

L-Theanine
L-Theanine: an amino acid found in the leaves used to make green and black teas. It promotes activity in the alpha frequency band; this frequency of brain waves is characteristic  of attention and relaxation of the mind without drowsiness. L-theanine promotes focused attention, mental alertness, and calm, relaxed energy.\

L-Ornithine
L-Ornithine is an amino acid. It can be produced in the human body, but is also supplemented by human diet, through both animal and vegan sources including meats, nuts, rice, eggs, and fish (ours is vegan-sourced). Ornithine can be taken up by the brain and is found in many other tissues, with its highest concentrations found in connective tissues, such as the skin. L-ornithine is able to influence a number of brain functions, hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis signaling, as well as GABA and glutamate pathways.
## Learn More About The Other 8 Ingredients
+
Vitamin B12 (as methylcobalamin): Vitamin B12 is a B-complex vitamin and a brain essential nutrient. Methylcobalamin is the coenzyme (i.e., active) form of vitamin B12, and the preferred nootropic form. It supports the healthy function of brain and nerve cells, and is needed for the production of myelin. Methylcobalamin is involved in promoting healthy dopamine, melatonin, noradrenaline, and serotonin, and supports healthy hearing and vision.
Pantothenic Acid (as calcium pantothenate): Pantothenic acid (B5) is a B-complex vitamin and a brain essential nutrient. It supports healthy brain energy production, the synthesis of neurotransmitters (most notably acetylcholine), the production of the myelin required for healthy nerve function, and adrenal function (it’s often referred to as the “anti-stress” vitamin).\
Vitamin B6 (as pyridoxal 5'-phosphate): Vitamin B6 is a member of the B-complex vitamin family and a brain essential nutrient. Pyridoxal 5'-phosphate is a coenzyme (i.e., active) form of vitamin B6. It supports the healthy production of brain glucose and energy metabolism. Vitamin B6 is directly or indirectly needed for supporting the production of neurotransmitters related to motivation, focus, and mood, including acetylcholine, dopamine, GABA, and serotonin.\
Niacin (as Niacinamide): Niacinamide (B3) is a member of the B-complex vitamin family and a brain essential nutrient. It supports healthy NAD+ levels, energy production, antioxidant defenses, and cellular repair and stress response functions. Vitamin B3 is critical for maintaining the health of the central nervous system (i.e., it’s neuroprotective).\
Riboflavin: Riboflavin (B2) is a member of the B-complex vitamin family and a brain essential nutrient. It supports optimal brain energy production, the production of glutathione (a cellular antioxidant), and healthy adrenal function. Riboflavin is needed for myelin and is used in the metabolism of fats, which is critical in the brain since it is mostly made of fats.\
Thiamine (as thiamine HCl): Thiamine (B1) is a member of the B-complex vitamin family and a brain essential nutrient. It supports healthy brain energy metabolism, neuron health, neurotransmission, and adrenal gland function. Thiamin helps synthesize several neurotransmitters involved in focus, memory, motivation, and mood, including acetylcholine, dopamine, and GABA.\
Folate (as L-5'-methyltetrahydrofolate calcium salt) is the primary biologically active form of folate or vitamin B9. Other forms of this vitamin include folic acid (used in food fortification and most supplements) and folinic acid (also called calcium folinate). Folates got their name from the Latin word for leaf (folium), because leafy green vegetables (e.g., lettuce, spinach) are one of their better food sources. Beans, lentils, nuts, and seeds are also good sources. The form we use, L-5-MTHF, is the folate form found in circulation and transported across the blood–brain barrier. Folate is a central player in a process called methylation or methyl donation, a critical component of optimized metabolic function.
Caffeine is a well known methylxanthine compound used to combat fatigue and promote alertness. It’s found in seeds, fruits, nuts, and leaves of a number of plants native to Africa, East Asia, and South America. These include coffee beans (as well as coffee cherry fruits), cocoa beans, guarana berries, kola nuts, and the leaves from tea, guayusa, and yerba mate. The 100mg’s in a serving of Qualia Focus is similar to an average cup of coffee. Caffeine can help us perform physically and mentally by promoting healthy arousal (wakefulness), which is an important part of being able to pay attention and react quickly. Not surprisingly, this has led to caffeine being one of the most widely used and studied substances for supporting both sports performance and brain function.

What’s the difference between Qualia Focus and Qualia Mind?

Qualia Focus and Qualia Mind are both known for their fast-acting, science-backed nootropic ingredients designed to enhance similar areas of cognitive function, such as alertness, motivation, energy, and memory. While Qualia Focus provides excellent value and serves as a strong standalone nootropic, Qualia Mind delivers a premium experience with more comprehensive cognitive support. It also extends its benefits to support executive functions, emotional resilience, and social cognition. \
How should I take Qualia Focus?

We recommend taking Qualia Focus 1-2 hours after waking, with a large glass of water.
How soon will I feel the nootropic benefits associated with Qualia Focus?
+
Qualia Focus contains several fast-acting nootropic ingredients, such as caffeine, American ginseng, L-ornithine, and L-theanine. These ingredients are often noticeable the same day they’re taken. During pre-launch testing, participants commonly reported increased alertness and energy within 30–60 minutes of taking a dose. Most users experienced benefits in areas like focus, motivation, productivity, and stress resilience within the first week of using Qualia Focus.\☨
How long will the nootropic benefits associated with Qualia Focus last?

Qualia Focus was designed to amplify the nootropic effects of caffeine—such as improved alertness, energy, attention, and mood—while mitigating common downsides like jitteriness and the crash that can occur an hour or several hours later. In pre-launch testing of Qualia Focus, around 60% of participants reported sustained nootropic benefits lasting 4 to 8 hours, and 77% (nearly 8 out of 10) experienced noticeable effects for at least several hours.\☨

FAQs
What is magnesium glycinate good for?
+
Magnesium glycinate is well known for supporting relaxation, muscle recovery, and healthy sleep. Because it is bound to glycine, it is typically gentle on digestion and highly bioavailable, making it a popular choice for those who want calming support without laxative effects. It may also help ease muscle tension and support a balanced stress response. Qualia Magnesium+ includes ten highly absorbable forms of magnesium, including magnesium glycinate, to promote relaxation, recovery, and nervous system support.\
What is the best time to take magnesium?
+
The exact time of day you take magnesium is less important than establishing a consistent time and pattern for taking magnesium that fits your routine and supports your main health goals. For example, taking a product like Qualia Magnesium+ in the evening with dinner is popular because its blend of bioavailable magnesium forms (including magnesium glycinate) can help support relaxation, less muscle tension , and restful sleep, while others find a morning dose with food supports daily metabolic health. You can also split your dose into morning and evening. Consistency and taking it with food often enhances absorption and allows for easier digestion.\
Is magnesium bisglycinate the same as magnesium glycinate?
+
Yes, magnesium bisglycinate and magnesium glycinate refer to the same highly bioavailable magnesium form which is chelated with the amino acid glycine. "Bisglycinate" technically indicates two glycine molecules bound to each magnesium ion. “Glycinate” is often used as a shorthand on supplement labels. This form of magnesium is noted for excellent absorption, enhancing relaxation, sleep support, and nervous system balance. In high-quality supplements like Qualia Magnesium+, magnesium bisglycinate chelate (often labeled as magnesium glycinate) is included alongside other magnesium forms to help maximize uptake and support everything from calm and recovery to metabolic and bone health.\
What’s the bioavailability of Qualia Magnesium+?
+
When we created Qualia Magnesium+, our goal was to include forms of magnesium that have been proven by research as well-retained and usable by the body. Contrary to popular belief, we don’t take magnesium because it is bioavailable (that’s a feature not a benefit); we take it to support optimizing tissue levels, which promotes health. It’s not just the amount of magnesium that goes in your mouth that matters: it’s what gets to your cells. The ten forms of magnesium in Qualia Magnesium+ are backed by research that they are more effective than others when it comes to optimizing magnesium in different tissues.\
What are the benefits of magnesium?
+
Magnesium is an essential mineral in the body, supporting the activity of over 600 enzymes by acting as a cofactor, and is involved in most major biochemical and metabolic pathways in our cells. A few of the most common benefits of Qualia Magnesium+ include support for balanced moods, easing tense muscles, promoting restful sleep, soothing feelings of stress, and facilitating recovery from exercise.\ Learn more about the benefits of magnesium.
How should I get started with Qualia Magnesium+?
+
Our general recommendation for those new to magnesium supplementation is to take 1-2 capsules with water or your preferred beverage, accompanied by food. Qualia Magnesium+ can be taken at any time of day that best fits your schedule. Some individuals prefer taking magnesium in the morning with breakfast or at lunch, while others find it more convenient to take it with dinner. It's also perfectly fine to split the dose, taking one capsule with breakfast and another with dinner. The key is to choose a time that best fits your daily routine, ensuring consistent use of your magnesium supplement for optimal benefits.\
How soon should I see benefits from Qualia Magnesium+?
+
Magnesium supplements aren’t necessarily noticed or felt.The perception of benefits can vary widely among individuals, influenced by factors such as initial magnesium levels, dietary habits, exercise routines, sleep patterns, stress levels, medication use, and other factors. Magnesium supplements gradually enhance tissue magnesium status over time, so benefits, when noticed, tend to be gradual and occur over several weeks. This gradual improvement aligns with magnesium’s role in supporting overall health and well-being, even if the effects aren't always consciously felt.\

Do nootropics like Qualia Mind support cognitive performance?
+
Yes. Qualia Mind supports motivation, energy, focus, memory, and productivity. These are the primary reasons people take nootropics, and are fundamental to cognitive performance. The most robust type of evidence that nootropics support cognitive performance comes from high-quality scientific studies in which the effect of a given compound is compared to that of a placebo control. Qualia Mind contains nootropic compounds that have been studied in placebo-controlled trials for supporting cognitive performance.\
Why Do You Recommend Taking Qualia Mind 5 Days a Week, Followed by 2 Off Days?
+
Qualia Life Sciences applies complex systems science to its approach. A key aspect of this model is adaptation—living organisms are highly skilled at adjusting to their surroundings. With this in mind, Qualia's formulations are intended to be taken for five consecutive days, followed by a two-day break each week. This schedule helps optimize the effects while preventing the body from becoming desensitized. The days off can be consecutive or spread out, as long as the break occurs weekly.
What are the most potent nootropics?
+
Caffeine is one of the most potent and studied nootropics. Caffeine is a brain stimulant that promotes alertness and enhances attention, reaction time, and information processing. Aging well (or poorly) begins at the cellular level, from DNA repair, to mitochondrial function, to the production of energy. How well we support these processes plays a large role in the trajectory of how well we age for decades to come.
Ancient Wisdom
We researched and included ingredients such as Gynostemma pentaphyllum, known as the “immortality herb” for centuries in Asian cultures, and rosemary leaf extract, a health-promoting plant used for centuries in Greek tradition.
Impeccable Ingredient Sourcing
All ingredients in Qualia Mitochondria+ are ethically sourced, gluten free, and 3rd party tested. Additionally, many ingredients in Qualia Mitochondria+ are trademarked as standardized in concentrated forms to ensure maximal potency and benefit.\
Relationships Between Ingredients
Our science team pioneered the use of complex systems science in supplement formulations, which values the sum total effect of different ingredients in combination with each other, including deep study of their ideal dosage relationships, to achieve the greatest health benefits.\
Qualia Mitochondria+ Ingredients
Gynostemma pentaphyllum.
Despite the common name “Southern Ginseng”, this herb is not closely related to Panax ginseng. And until recently, it was primarily only known to cultures in the mountainous regions of southern China and northern Vietnam, where it’s often called the "immortality herb,” and regularly consumed in jiaogulan herbal tea for its longevity benefits. Modern science confirms its ability to support mitochondrial and metabolic health.\
Sensoril® Ashwagandha Extract
Ashwaganda’s ability to support stress resistance, mitochondrial function, and exercise performance, has made this once-obscure Ayurvedic herb increasingly popular. But its potency and purity depend on how and where it is sourced. We use Sensoril® Ashwagandha root and leaf extract, which has standardized withanolide glycoside content (a group of plant compounds responsible for many of ashwagandha's most profound health effects).\
Black Ginger (Kaempferia parviflora)
Kaempferia parviflora, commonly known as black ginger or Thai ginseng, is found in the northeastern regions of Thailand, where its root extracts have long been used as a health tonic and energy enhancer. Its novel active constituents (a special type of polyphenol called polymethoxyflavonoids) are standardized in Sirtmax® Kaempferia parviflora, which we use in the formula to support mitochondrial function and healthy aging.\
BioVin® French Red Grapes Extract
Grape skins and seeds contain trans-resveratrol, which has been subject to over 100 clinical studies, renowned for supporting healthy aging. But grapes have more than one relevant compound: resveratrol derivatives (such as viniferins and polydatin) and polyphenol compounds (such as quercetin and catechins) have complementary relationships with trans-resveratrol. That’s why we use BioVin®, with 5% standardized trans-resveratrol, and full-spectrum inclusion of juice, seeds, and skins.\
Cocoa Seed Extract (Theobroma cacao)
Theobroma cacao or “cocoa trees” have been cultivated in central and South America for at least 3,000 years. The dark chocolate made from its seeds or “beans” have a growing reputation for supporting heart and brain health. Cocoa seeds are one of the best dietary sources of health-promoting polyphenol compounds. They are an especially great food source of a type of polyphenol called cocoa flavanols.\ We use ACTICOA® cocoa, standardized at 7.5% cocoa flavanols content, to ensure quality.
ProcynCi® Cinnamomum cassia Bark Extract
Cinnamon comes from the inner bark of several different tree species from the genus Cinnamomum. The species Cinnamomum cassia has been the most studied, for its class of bioactive molecules called procyanidins, a type of polyphenol particularly important to supporting health benefits such as healthy aging and metabolic function.\ We use ProcynCi® Cinnamomum cassia Bark Extract, which is standardized to contain no less than 40% polyphenols, and 6% procyanidins.
Japanese Pagoda Tree Flower Extract
The dried flower buds of the Japanese Pagoda Tree (Sophorae japonica L.) contain as much as 20% rutin. Our flower extract contains 95% rutin, which is renowned for its ability to support cellular responses, antioxidant defenses, and mitochondrial function. Rutin also promotes healthy aging by supporting AMPK signaling, SIRT-1 signaling, healthy neural immune signaling, and countering oxidative stress.\
Pomanox® Pomegranate Fruit Extract
Pomegranate (Punica granatum) is a fruit-bearing tree native to Iran, Northern India and Afghanistan. Pomegranate is known for its fruit, a round berry with a thick peel filled with arils with seeds. The arils have a deep red color resulting from their high polyphenol content, including punicalagins, the most potent antioxidant in pomegranate. We use Pomanox®, a patented extract of pomegranate whole fruit standardized to contain not less than 30% Punicalagins (A+B) and Punicalins (A+B).
## Learn More About The Other 26 Ingredients
+
PuremidineTM Spermidine (as spermidine trihydrochloride): Spermidine is a polyamine found in many food sources such as wheat germ, nuts, mushrooms, and fermented products. It’s well known for supporting autophagy (which can be thought of as a cell's natural recycling program). Spermidine also offers support for healthy stress response, DNA stability, brain health, and tissue repair.\ We use PuremidineTM which is standardized for ≥98% spermidine trihydrochloride.
Pantothenic acid (vitamin B5): is part of the B complex—a group of water-soluble vitamins that play important roles in cellular metabolism and energy production. It is an essential vitamin and the precursor of Coenzyme A (CoA), a molecule that is ubiquitous in the human body and that participates in the key metabolic pathways for cellular energy generation. Vitamin B5 supports healthy adrenal function, which is why it is sometimes described as an “anti-stress” vitamin.\
Coenzyme Q10 (CoQ10): is an important fat-soluble nutrient, because it’s essential for cellular energy production (i.e., ATP) and antioxidant defenses, helping protect membranes from oxidative stress. The best vegetarian sources are foods high in fat, including nuts, seeds, avocados, and vegetable oils, but most CoQ10 is made by the body rather than being from diet, and its production often declines with age.
Lipoic acid (R-lipoic acid or alpha-lipoic acid): is an important mitochondrial compound because it’s used in helper molecules (i.e., enzymes) for the metabolic processes that convert food energy into cellular energy (ATP). The richest food sources are organ meats (e.g., kidney, liver) and vegetables such as spinach and broccoli. Many animal and human studies report functional benefits when diets are supplemented with extra lipoic acid.\ We use Bio-Enhanced®, Stabilized R-Lipoic Acid (sodium R-alpha-lipoic acid), the most bioavailable and potent form of lipoic acid.
Pyrroloquinoline quinone (PQQ): is thought of as a non-vitamin growth factor, supporting healthy metabolism and gene expression. PQQ is often categorized as a mitochondrial nutrient, supporting mitochondrial efficiency, so they are more capable of converting dietary fats and sugars into cellular energy. PQQ also plays roles in promoting a healthy gut microbiome, immune system function, antioxidant defenses, and cognitive function. In the brain, it appears to be especially important in supporting healthy memory and cognition with aging. Some of the best food sources include spinach, parsley, and kiwifruit.\
Magnesium (as Aquamin® magnesium hydroxide): is one of the most abundant minerals in the body and plays a vital role in supporting the function of all living cells. It’s used in more than 300 enzymes. ATP (i.e., cellular energy) occurs complexed with magnesium, so all enzymes utilizing ATP require magnesium to support their activity. Because magnesium supports the electrical functions of cells, it’s necessary for muscle and nerve function. Aquamin® Mg is a bioavailable source of magnesium that also contains lesser amounts of 71 other minerals, and it has been studied for supporting gut microbiota.\
L-cystine: is the chief sulfur-containing compound in protein. It is found in eggs, meat, dairy products, and whole grain foods. Inside cells, L-cystine is metabolized into L-cysteine, which, along with glutamine and glycine, is used to make an important detoxification and antioxidant molecule called glutathione. L-cystine has important structure and function roles in immune cells, skeletal muscle, and connective tissue.\
Betaine: is a compound originally found in sugar beets (Beta vulgaris), which is the source of its name. Betaine plays a central role in methylation and is involved in the synthesis of important neurotransmitters. It also supports aspects of sports performance, NAD metabolism, and brain health.\
L-Tryptophan: is an essential amino acid. Many recognize it as the compound in turkey known for inducing sleepiness, but L-tryptophan is also found in abundance in many plants. Ours is vegan sourced, and L-Tryptophan is a precursor used by the body to make melatonin, as well as NAD+ and serotonin, supporting sleep and calm mood among many other health benefits.\
L-carnitine: (also known as levocarnitine) is an important molecule because it’s needed to convert fat into energy. The name carnitine is derived from Latin “carnus” (flesh), because it was originally found in meat extracts, though ours is vegan. L-carnitine’s most important role is in mitochondrial fat metabolism—it is used to transport long-chain fatty acids across the mitochondrial membrane for breakdown by mitochondrial β-oxidation. This transportation function allows fats and oils from our diet to be used for energy production.
Niacin (Vitamin B3): is a member of the B-complex vitamin family and a brain essential nutrient. It supports healthy NAD+ levels, energy production, antioxidant defenses, and cellular repair and stress response functions. Vitamin B3 is critical for maintaining the health of the central nervous system.\
Niacinamide (vitamin B3): Compounds with niacin activity are defined by their ability to contribute the nicotinamide (i.e., niacinamide) unit of an important molecule called NAD. NAD is found in every cell in the body. It's used to (1) make cellular energy (ATP), (2) protect cells from damage, and (3) activate processes linked to healthier aging. Research shows that as we age levels of some NAD metabolites (NAD+ as an example) decline substantially. Many healthy aging scientists and doctors believe maintaining higher levels of NAD+ is one of the keys to increasing the amount of time we spend in good health.\
Vitamin B12 (as methylcobalamin): Vitamin B12 is a B-complex vitamin and a brain essential nutrient. Methylcobalamin is the coenzyme or “active” form of vitamin B12, supporting the healthy function of brain and nerve cells, and is needed for the production of myelin. Methylcobalamin is involved in promoting healthy dopamine, melatonin, noradrenaline, serotonin, and supports healthy hearing and vision.\
Folates: encompass all the different forms of vitamin B9 (the ninth of the B-vitamins discovered). These include folic acid (used in food fortification and most supplements), folinic acid (also called calcium folinate) and L-5'-methyltetrahydrofolate. Folates got their name from the Latin word for leaf (folium), because many leafy green vegetables are rich in folates. They are critical for the production and maintenance of new cells, playing a key role in DNA expression and repair.
Citrus sinensis L. Fruit Extract: includes sweet oranges and blood oranges. The peels and fruit are a rich source of nobiletin, a citrus flavonoid that supports healthy blood vessels and veins, and promotes antioxidant defenses and a balanced cellular response. We use an extract with a particularly potent 40% nobiletin standardization. Nobiletin also supports circadian rhythm among its many other notable health benefits.\
Apigenin (from Citrus grandis fruit extract): is a flavone found in many fruits and vegetables. It has been reported to support cardiovascular, brain, and kidney function, with metabolic benefits. It indirectly supports NAD+ via the CD38 NAD+-consuming pathway. Apigenin is also supportive of antioxidant defenses and the mitochondrial cellular energy network.\
Rosemary Leaf Extract (Rosmarinus Officinalis): is a member of the mint family, and contains a range of health-supporting polyphenols, including diterpenes (such as carnosol, carnosic acid, rosmarinic acid) and a triterpene called ursolic acid. Triterpenes are produced by plants as part of their self-defense mechanism. Ursolic acid benefits include support for the body’s healthy response to exercise (antioxidant defenses, insulin balance, sirtuin function, and AMPK activity). It also supports the development of new muscle fibers and muscle rejuvenation.\
Creatine monohydrate: plays a key role in tissues, like muscles and the brain, that use high amounts of energy. Because it concentrates in muscles, the best food sources for creatine are red meat, pork, lamb, poultry, and fish. So creatine as a dietary supplement for vegans and vegetarians should be considered (such as vegan-friendly creatine monohydrate). Creatine is used in the phosphocreatine (phosphagen) system. This system regenerates ATP from ADP in tissues, and is especially important in circumstances with high energy demand.\
Vitamin K2 (as K2VITAL® Menaquanone-7): Vitamin K is a collective term for a group of structurally related fat-soluble molecules (vitamers) that act as a cofactor for a carboxylase enzyme. This enzyme transforms glutamate residues in proteins to carboxyglutamate residues, which plays an important role in blood clotting and bone health. Dietary forms of vitamin K fall into two categories: (1) vitamin K1 (phylloquinone), which is obtained from vegetables, and (2) vitamin K2 (menaquinone), which is obtained from products of animal origin or fermented foods. Ours is vegan sourced as K2VITAL® Menaquanone-7, a particularly bioavailable form.\
Inositol : used to be considered part of the B-complex of vitamins (it was called vitamin B8), but because we can make it in the body, it’s no longer classified as a vitamin. In the body, inositol is found in cell membrane phospholipids, plasma lipoproteins, and (as the phosphate form) in the cell nucleus (the home of our DNA). We use inositol in this formula to support healthy hormone signaling—insulin and thyroid especially. Inositol acts as a second messenger, translating hormone messages that act on the outside of cells into intracellular signals involved in energy production, growth, and repair.\
Riboflavin Riboflavin (B2): is a member of the B-complex vitamin family and a brain essential nutrient. It supports optimal brain energy production, the production of glutathione (a cellular antioxidant), and healthy adrenal function. Riboflavin is needed for myelin and is used in the metabolism of fats, which is critical in the brain since it is mostly made of fats.\
Vitamin B6 (as pyridoxal 5'-phosphate): Vitamin B6 is a member of the B-complex vitamin family and a brain essential nutrient. Pyridoxal 5'-phosphate is a coenzyme (i.e., active) form of vitamin B6. It supports the healthy production of brain glucose and energy metabolism. Vitamin B6 is directly or indirectly needed for supporting the production of neurotransmitters related to motivation, focus, and mood, and also supports general immune health.\
Vitamin B1 (Thiamine):  is a member of the B-complex vitamin family and a brain essential nutrient. It supports healthy brain energy metabolism, neuron health, neurotransmission, and adrenal gland function. It also supports energy metabolism and metabolic health.\
Biotin: or vitamin B7, is part of the B complex—a group of water-soluble vitamins that play important roles in cellular metabolism and energy production. Biotin was originally called vitamin H, with “H” standing in for Haar und Haut, German words for hair and skin. Biotin is an important cofactor in some enzymes involved in metabolizing fats and carbohydrates, influencing cell growth, and affecting amino acids involved in protein synthesis, and supports both gene expression and cellular metabolic pathways.\
Vitamin B12 (adenosylcobalamin): is unique among vitamins because it contains a metal ion, cobalt, from which the term cobalamin derived. Adenosylcobalamin is one of the two coenzyme forms of vitamin B12 (the other is methylcobalamin). These are the forms used in enzymes in the human body. Adenosylcobalamin is used in only one enzyme, L-methyl-malonyl-CoA mutase.

What’s the difference between Qualia Mitochondria+ and Qualia Senolytic?

Qualia Mitochondria+ is designed to support cellular energy production and maintain the health of cells as we age and is intended to be used on most days of the month. What’s the difference between Qualia Mitochondria+ and Qualia NAD+?

Why Do You Recommend Taking Qualia Mitochondria 5 Days a Week, Followed by 2 Off Days?

NAD+ is in every living cell of our bodies. It’s vital for characteristics of a body in its prime, such as cellular energy production and metabolism, and even daily DNA repair. But by age 40, most people’s NAD+ levels are \half\ of what they were at age 20. Qualia NAD+ is clinically proven to boost NAD+ levels by 67%, rejuvenating levels of this indispensable molecule for healthy aging.\†
The Science (And Art) Of Boosting NAD+ By 67%
What is the best way to boost NAD+ levels in aging adults? That has been an ongoing scientific challenge for decades. NAD+ is a large molecule, so supplementing it directly has limited bioavailability. To bypass this challenge, our science team focused on compounds the body can readily convert into NAD+, known as NAD+ precursors, such as niacin, niacinamide, and nicotinamide riboside (NR, in the form of NIAGEN). All three are in Qualia NAD+, along with 11 other ingredients which play crucial roles in supporting the entirety of the body’s NAD+ production process. \ Key focuses of our formulation include:
A Holistic Approach
Even with 3 NAD+ precursors in our formula, there’s other aspects of NAD+ production underlying the clinical results of Qualia NAD+, such as a highly bioavailable B-vitamin complex to support ATP production, and its critical relationship to NAD+ production.\

Relationships Between Ingredients

Qualia NAD+ Ingredients
NIAGEN®
NIAGEN® (Nicotinamide Riboside) is a patented, clinically studied, next-generation vitamin B3 with 300+ clinical studies. It differs from niacin and niacinamide (the first-generation forms of vitamin B3) because it contains more of the full NAD+ molecule and is metabolized into NAD+ differently. It is considered an NAD+ precursor as it is a direct precursor in the biosynthesis pathway of NAD+. NIAGEN® has been clinically studied to boost NAD+.\
Niacinamide
Niacinamide (vitamin B3) is a NAD+ precursor that is part of the B complex—a group of water-soluble vitamins that play important roles in cellular metabolism and energy production. It is called B3 because it was the third of the B complex vitamins to be discovered. It is on the World Health Organization's List of Essential Medicines, as one of the most effective and safe nutrients needed in a health system.\
Nicotinic acid
Nicotinic acid (vitamin B3). The term niacin is often used as a synonym for this form of vitamin B3. It is considered an NAD+ precursor because compounds with niacin activity are defined by their ability to contribute to the nicotinamide unit of an NAD+, critical for mitochondrial energy production, cellular repair and signaling, and cellular defenses. Nicotinic acid makes NAD via the Preiss-Handler pathway. \

Coffeeberry® is made from organic coffee fruits, which are often called coffee cherries. Like cherries, coffee plants produce soft red fruits surrounding a pit or hard seed. B-Complex Vitamins (from Saccharomyces cerevisiae culture) is an inactivated yeast that contains standardized amounts of all eight B vitamins (and thus 8 ingredients within the Qualia NAD+ formula): thiamine (B1), riboflavin (B2), niacin (B3), pantothenic acid (B5), pyridoxine (B6), biotin (B7), folic acid (B9), and cobalamin (B12). FAQs
What are NAD+ boosting supplements?
+
NAD+ boosting supplements promote the production of NAD+ in your cells and boost the levels of NAD+ in your body. They may do so by providing precursors for NAD+ production (such as NIAGEN® nicotinamide riboside, niacin, and niacinamide) and/or other compounds that support the pathways that transform precursors into the NAD+ molecule.\
What does NAD+ do for the body?
+
NAD+ plays a key part in the production of cellular energy as ATP, and consequently, in powering cellular functions that rely on ATP (a lot of cellular work requires ATP). NAD+ is also needed for cells to respond appropriately to metabolic stress and repair damage, both of which are needed for maintaining proper tissue function and health. \
How does NAD+ impact aging?
+
NAD+ levels decline naturally as we age. This decline is believed to impact several of the proposed Hallmarks of Aging, including functions related to mitochondrial performance, genetic repair mechanisms, and stem cell renewal. Maintaining or boosting NAD+ levels has the potential to support the cellular processes that rely on NAD+. It is a way to address some of the effects aging has on the trillions of cells in our body.\
Can you take Qualia NAD+ every day?
+
Yes. We recommend taking Qualia NAD+ daily in the morning.\
Is Qualia NAD+ clinically tested?
+
Yes. A double-blind, placebo-controlled clinical trial of Qualia NAD+ was carried out with healthy individuals aged 40 to 64 years old. Preliminary results from 12 participants who have completed the study have shown a statistically significant increase in NAD+ levels compared to placebo. NAD+ increased on average 74% after taking Qualia NAD+ once daily for 4 weeks.\†
Full Study Results
Why does Qualia NAD+ have three NAD precursors?
+
NAD+ is so important to cellular function that there’s more than one way to make it. Qualia NAD+ combines three NAD+ precursors— NIAGEN® nicotinamide riboside, niacin, and niacinamide—to support the redundancy cells rely on to make NAD+. Cells use these precursors in different ways to produce NAD+. We wanted to make sure cells had a supply of each.\
How do I get started with Qualia NAD+?
+
You can get started with Qualia NAD+ simply by taking 2 capsules with water or a beverage of your choice in the morning, with or without food. We recommend taking Qualia NAD+ at the beginning of your day because circadian (i.e., body clock) research in animals suggests that NAD+ boosting compounds offer better support for metabolic health when taken in the morning. Taking Qualia NAD+ at the start of your day makes the best use of these natural rhythms.\
Does Qualia NAD+ cause flushing?
+
Qualia NAD+ contains the nicotinic acid (niacin) form of vitamin B3 that can cause flushing when taken in amounts substantially above the daily value (DV), particularly when taken on an empty stomach. Although the amount of nicotinic acid in Qualia NAD+ is below the level at which flushing usually occurs, the possibility of Qualia NAD+ causing flushing cannot be ruled out. The other forms of vitamin B3 included in Qualia NAD+ (niacinamide and NIAGEN® Nicotinamide Riboside) do not cause flushing.\†
How does Qualia NAD+ compare to NAD+ IV therapy?
+
Qualia NAD+ is an NAD+-boosting dietary supplement that provides several precursors for NAD+ production along with compounds that support rate-limiting steps in NAD+ biosynthesis. Qualia NAD+ was designed to support intracellular levels of NAD+.\ NAD+ IV therapy consists of the administration of NAD+ directly into the bloodstream, which means it primarily boosts extracellular NAD+.

70% Of Us Struggle With Poor Sleep.
That’s according to recent surveys tracking Americans’ sleep quality. A combination of factors, from digital devices late at night, to less lifestyle relationship with sunlight, to the chaotic stressors of our modern world, all play a role.
Poor sleep contributes to weaker immune function, weight gain, poor cardiovascular health, and accelerated aging, among countless other health detriments. But recapturing great sleep naturally requires nuanced nutrition that supports the totality of sleep health, from healthy circadian rhythms, to homeostatic sleep drive, and the body’s own melatonin production. These factors (and many others) informed the research behind Qualia Night.
Ancient Wisdom
Ingredients in Qualia Night such as saffron have been used in Asian medicine traditions for centuries to support sleep, and parts of the hawthorne plant such as the extract in Qualia Night, have long been used in European herbal traditions to induce calm and manage stress.
Impeccable Ingredient Sourcing
All ingredients in Qualia Night are ethically sourced and gluten free. But additionally, many ingredients in Qualia Night are trademarked as standardized and concentrated forms - to ensure maximal potency and benefit.
Relationships Between Ingredients
Our science team pioneered the use of complex systems science in supplement formulations, which values the sum total effect of different ingredients in combination with each other, including deep study of their ideal dosage relationships, to achieve the greatest health benefits.
Qualia Night Ingredients
Holy Basil (Ocimum sanctum) Leaf Extract
Holy Basil (Ocimum sanctum) is an aromatic herb that has been used within Ayurvedic medicine for more than 3000 years. Now categorized as an adaptogen, holy basil has been researched to support a healthy stress response, mood, cognition and immune function.
Reishi Mushroom
Often called “the mushroom of immortality”, Ganoderma lucidum (commonly known as Reishi mushroom), has been used for centuries in Traditional Chinese Medicine under the name “Lingzhi” in shen tonic, which was used to promote peace of mind, sound sleep, and clear thinking. Modern research on reishi mushrooms also indicates support for sleep, as well as immune function.
VINEATROL®20 Grapevine Extract
Grapevine shoot extract has wide-ranging health benefits for sleep (and beyond). We’ve chosen the standardized version of VINEATROL®20 Grapevine Extract because it contains at least 5% trans-resveratrol and no less than 20% resveratrol monomers and oligomers. It supports brain function and neuroprotective functions, which are key considerations when formulating for mental (and not just physical) recovery while sleeping.
KSM-66 Ashwagandha®
Ashwaganda (also known as indian ginseng) has rapidly growing appreciation for its profound health benefits, which include sleep quality. But ashwagandha quality can vary. We use KSM-66 Ashwagandha®, an award-winning standardized full-spectrum ashwagandha root extract backed by 14 years of R&D, which supports sleep onset and total sleep time, sleep efficiency, quality of sleep, and slow wave sleep.\
Saffron Stigma Extract
Saffron is a spice derived from the flowers of Crocus sativus. It’s been used and traded as a spice for at least 4000 years. The saffron we use is standardized for 0.3% safranal, a compound within saffron that contributes to many of its health benefits. Saffron supports calm mood, positive mental-emotional bias, GABA-glutamate signaling, and sleep.
Gotu Kola (Centella asiatica ) Whole Herb Extract
In Ayurvedic medicine, Gotu Kola has long been regarded as a mental rejuvenator which counters mental fatigue and improves thinking. Modern research has shown it’s an ideal nootropic towards the end of the day because it calms, and supports repair and rejuvenation processes related to cognitive function. Gotu kola contains a bioactive compound called asiaticoside critical to many of its health benefits, and our Gotu kola is standardized to contain no less than 10% asiaticosides.
White Peony (Paeonia lactiflora) Root Extract
The peeled roots of white peony (Paeonia lactiflora) are one of the most common herbs used in Traditional Chinese Medicine, where some of its uses included supporting sleep, emotional stability, mental focus, and immunity. It contains a compound called paeoniflorin that in preclinical research influences adenosine signaling—a molecule involved in sleep homeostatic drive.
Wild Blueberry Fruit Extract
Unlike commercial blueberries, wild blueberries (Vaccinium angustifolium) are smaller and have more intense color than cultivated “highbush” blueberries. They also have higher anthocyanin and polyphenols content, both key to blueberry’s overall health benefits. Wild blueberry supplementation has been studied for supporting cognitive function and brain health. It also supports exercise performance and muscle recovery.
## Learn More About The Other 24 Ingredients
+
Polygala tenuifolia Root Extract: An herb used for centuries in Traditional Chinese Medicine with preclinical research suggesting it supports brain protection and repair processes including molecules such as BDNF and NGF, counters chronic stress, supports sleep, and influences both adenosine signaling—a molecule involved in the sleep homeostatic drive—and GABA signaling—a neurotransmitter involved with relaxation at night and sleep
Uridine Monophosphate: A nootropic compound that is a building block of the main information carrying molecules in the body (DNA and RNA), and plays a central role in cellular metabolism. It’s a natural sleep-promoting substance made by the brain, acting via uridine receptors in the areas of the brain which regulate natural sleep.
Astapure® Whole Algae Powder is a branded Haematococcus pluvialis microalgae extract standardized for astaxanthin content. There is a growing body of research on astaxanthin for cognitive function support, immune system support, skin health, vision and other areas of wellness and performance. Its chemical structure allows it to readily cross the blood-brain barrier.
Lycopene is one member in a class of natural pigments called carotenoids—carotenoids are fat-soluble yellow, orange, or red pigments. Lycopene is on the red end of the carotenoid pigment color continuum. Lycopene can’t be synthesized in the body, so must be obtained from the diet, in abundance in sources such as tomatoes. It supports healthy brain function, antioxidant defenses, the gut microbiota, and healthy aging.
Betaine is a compound originally found in sugar beets (Beta vulgaris), which is the source of its name. Betaine plays a central role in methylation and is involved in the synthesis of important neurotransmitters (dopamine and serotonin) and the neurohormone melatonin, making it an important contributor in the formula to support natural melatonin production.
L-theanine is a calming amino acid found in green and black tea leaves shown to support sleep, stress resilience, cognitive function, relaxed mood, and general immune health.
L-Tryptophan is an essential amino acid. Many recognize it as the compound in turkey known for inducing sleepiness, but L-tryptophan is also found in abundance in many plants. Ours is vegan sourced, and L-Tryptophan is a precursor used by the body to make melatonin, as well as NAD+ and serotonin, supporting sleep and calm mood among many other health benefits.
Magnesium glycinate is a chelated form of the mineral magnesium, renowned for its sleep supporting benefits, including healthy EEG and neuroendocrine responses during sleep. It also supports mood and cellular health, but has far-ranging benefits beyond just sleep and sleep recovery, including support for musculoskeletal health and cardiovascular function.
Gamma-oryzanol (γ-oryzanol) is a mixture of lipid molecules called steryl ferulates, found in food sources such as rice bran. It supports neuroprotective functions, and also supports healthy behavioral and physiological responses to stress and non-rapid eye movement (NREM) sleep.
Quercetin is a yellow plant pigment. Its name is derived from the Latin word for oak forest—quercetum—because it was originally identified in oaks. We source ours from the bud of of the Sophora japonica plant. Quercetin supports an extremely long list of cognitive functions including motor activity, serotonin and dopamine signaling, noradrenaline signaling, brain mitochondrial function, as well as muscle recovery and joint health.
Grapefruit Whole Fruit Extract derived from (Citrus × paradisi) is a hybrid citrus fruit. One grapefruit ancestor is pomelo and the other is sweet orange—the × in the botanical name indicates its hybrid origin. It is rich in antioxidants, and also contains a flavonoid called naringin that gives grapefruit its distinctive bitter taste and plays a key role to grapefruit’s health benefits. Ours is standardized to contain no less than 20% naringin, and supports GABA signaling, cardiovascular function, and various sleep processes.
Hawthorne Leaf & Flower Extract have a long history of use. The leaf and flowers of Hawthorn— Crataegus sp. such as C. monogyna and C. oxyacantha —was a heart tonic used as support for an aging heart in European herbal traditions. Other traditional uses include support for stress, nervousness, mental well-being and sleep. Modern research shows it supports cardiovascular health and calm mood.
Ziziphus Jujuba Seed Extract is native to many regions in South Asia. The fruits and seeds have been used in both Korean and Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) where they are believed to alleviate stress and were used to calm the mind and spirit. Its ability to support sleep and calm mood has been reiterated in modern research, with a multitude of cognitive support benefits, including the support of BDNF (Brain Derived Neurotrophic Factor) and healthy stress response.
Sesame Seed Extract is one of the two best food sources of lignans. Lignans are a category of polyphenols, and like other polyphenols, are broadly described as antioxidants. While sesame seeds have about 16 different lignans, the one that has received the most research is sesamin. In animal studies, sesamin has been neuroprotective and supports molecules like BDNF, which is involved in nervous system repair and growth. It has also countered changes in behavior caused by stress.
Olivex® Olive Fruit Extract is an olive fruit extract made from French olives (Olea europea), with about 380 pounds of olives needed to make 1 pound of the extract. This concentration is needed to create an olive fruit extract standardized for olive polyphenols. It is triple standardized for (1) hydroxytyrosol ≥6%; (2) tyrosol ≥1%; and (3) total polyphenols as tyrosol equivalents ≥15%, and supports healthy aging, brain function, gut microbiota, and antioxidant defenses.
Schisandra Chinensis Fruit Extract is a type of berry and one of the 50 Fundamental Herbs in Traditional Chinese Medicine. The primary active compounds in the berries are schisandra lignans. Schisandra Chinensis Fruit Extract supports sleep, endurance capacity, mood, stress resistance, and brain function.
Vitamin B6 (Pyridoxal 5'-phosphate) is the active form of vitamin B6, which is part of the B complex—a group of water-soluble vitamins that play important roles in cellular metabolism and energy production. It supports energy metabolism, brain function, and general immune health.

A Holistic Approach
A truly holistic gut health formula factors for a sustainable means of arriving at gut health improvements. We aimed to broadly support all aspects of a thriving gut microbiome, so health benefits would be reflective of better balance, harmony, and self-regulation to the underlying physiology of the gut.
Impeccable Ingredient Sourcing

Our science team pioneered the use of complex systems science in supplement formulations, which values the sum total effect of different ingredients in combination with each other, including deep study of their ideal dosage relationships, to achieve the greatest health benefits.
Qualia Probiotic+ Ingredients
Fermeric™ Fermented Turmeric
Fermeric™ Fermented Turmeric  A fermented turmeric containing inactive cells of Lactobacillus plantarum. It also contains compounds and metabolites produced during fermentation. The fermentation process used to make Fermeric™ enhances the antioxidant content of the turmeric and transforms health-promoting curcuminoids into forms that can be more easily absorbed.\ We chose our suggested amount to supply one billion cells of inactivated Lactobacillus plantarum. As a postbiotic, it has been mostly studied for supporting the gut-immune axis and the health of the gut barrier.\
Fibriss™ Organic Baobab Fruit Pulp Powder
Fibriss™ Organic Baobab Fruit Pulp Powder has a 3-in-1 gut microbiome action—insoluble fiber, soluble fiber, and fruit polyphenols. There is no such thing as a baobab plantation; every tree is community or family-owned and wild-harvested. Fibriss™ is certified organic and sustainably sourced from existing tree populations, benefiting local people and economies in the rural parts of Africa where it grows.
LactoSpore® Bacillus coagulans
LactoSpore® Bacillus coagulans One of our primary research goals was to find a probiotic that was also a psychobiotic, meaning a probiotic that powerfully enhances the gut-brain connection. This led us to LactoSpore® –a spore-forming probiotic that lives in the soil, sometimes called a sporebiotic. LactoSpore® has been sold since 1991 and is backed by strong science, its human placebo-controlled study was excellent for supporting both gut performance and mood\ 1. LactoSpore® is shelf-stable and doesn’t require refrigeration.
InstaKombu™ Kombucha Powder
InstaKombu™ Kombucha Powder is a prebiotic and postbiotic kombucha powder, combining fermented black tea with apple cider vinegar and a prebiotic fiber. The InstaKombu™ we are using starts with premium small-leaf black tea grown in the Alishan mountains of Taiwan. The tea is fermented for 2-3 weeks. Fermentation is stopped by adding apple cider vinegar. InstaKombu™ also contains organic acids, polyphenols, flavonoids, antioxidants, and glucuronic acid, all of which play supportive roles in the gut microbiome.\ InstaKombu™ is alcohol-free.
Berriotics™ Fermented Berry Blend
Berriotics™ Fermented Berry Blend is technically 10 ingredients in 1, as it is made from fermenting 10 species of berry—acerola, black currant, bilberry, cherry, chokeberry, cranberry, gooseberry, mulberry, raspberry and strawberry. Fermentation creates unique metabolites that were not in the berry blend prior to fermentation. And, fermentation “predigests” some of the starches and fibers found in the berries, breaking down many of them into smaller microfibers, which may be better food sources for our own gut microbiota 2.\ A serving of Berriotics™ contains hundreds of millions of intact inanimate microbial cells, as well as cell debris, metabolites and end products produced during the fermentation process, including small amounts of short-chain fatty acids (acetate and butyrate).
DigeZyme® Multi-Enzyme Compl
DigeZyme® Multi-Enzyme Complex delivers on the goal of supporting both the upper and lower GI tracts, our research led us to a multi-enzyme complex containing five digestive enzymes called DigeZyme®. It uses “microbial” or “plant-derived” enzymes giving broad enzyme support, and has been studied in humans. DigeZyme® contains five enzymes: α–Amylase to help break down starches; Cellulase to help break down plant cell walls in fruits and vegetables; Lipase to promote the breakdown of fats; Protease to help break down proteins; and Lactase to help break down the milk sugar (lactose) found in some dairy products\
## Learn More About The Other Ingredients
+
SmartSeed™ Celastrus paniculatus Seed Extract The Celastrus paniculatus plant, also known in South Asia as “the intellect tree”, has seeds used for centuries as a brain tonic to support mental sharpness and memory. SmartSeed™ is a proprietary extract only found in Qualia products. It supports healthy acetylcholine signaling, which is an important neurotransmitter involved in attention, learning, memory, and neuroplasticity.\
N-Acetyl-D-Glucosamine (NAG) is a fermentable amino sugar shown to have several biological roles that support healthy gut-brain functions. It is part of some of the complex molecules in connective tissue, myelin, human breast milk, and mucus and is essential for building molecules used as food by some friendly bacteria. NAG-containing oligosaccharides were first identified more than 50 years ago as the 'bifidus factor' in human breast milk, supporting the growth of intestinal bifidobacteria. Some of the beneficial bacteria that live in mucus rely on NAG and NAG-containing molecules.
Magnesium (from Aquamin® Mg) Magnesium is one of the most abundant minerals in the body and plays a vital role in supporting the function of all living cells. It’s used in more than 300 enzymes. All enzymes which use ATP (the molecule cells use as their primary energy source) require magnesium to support their activity. The same is true for enzymes that synthesize DNA and RNA, so magnesium is constantly involved in a broad range of vital health functions. Aquamin® Mg is a bioavailable source of magnesium that also contains lesser amounts of 71 other minerals and has been studied for supporting gut microbiota.
Solnul™ is a source of resistant starch obtained from potatoes. Resistant starch is starch that's resistant to digestion (our digestive enzymes don’t break it down), which allows it to be used as food for good gut bacteria. Starchy foods have been in the human diet for tens of thousands of years. The main difference today is that starchy food products are often refined to improve their taste, appearance, and texture. But highly refined starches lose nearly all their nutrients and fiber, depriving gut microbes of one of their favorite foods—resistant starch. Resistant starch reaches the colon undigested like insoluble fiber and thereby supporting digestive health.
Sunfiber® is a clinically studied soluble fiber containing 100% partially hydrolyzed guar gum (PHGG). It is sometimes called guar fiber or galactomannan fiber. The starting material for PHGG is guar beans. These are seeds of the guar plant (Cyamopsis tetragonolobus L.), which is native to western India. Guar gum is extracted from guar beans to produce a high-viscosity soluble fiber (viscosity means a fiber that forms a gel). High-viscosity fibers can support healthy bowel function—viscous fibers add bulk to stools—but are relatively poor prebiotics.\ Sunfiber® differs from guar gum because it has been partially hydrolyzed (i.e., partially broken down) with fermentation-derived enzymes from a probiotic fungus (Aspergillus niger), decreases its viscosity, making it a better prebiotic fiber than guar gum.
Bacillus subtilis is a spore-forming probiotic (sometimes described as a sporebiotic). It was first isolated in 1832. Bacillus subtilis is considered to be a soil-based organism (or SBO), because it can be found in the soil and in decaying plant matter (such as hay). Bacillus subtilis have also long been used in foods, particularly in the preparation of alkaline-fermented foods. Spore-forming probiotics, like Bacillus subtilis, can be thought of as being akin to seeds from plants that germinate and grow when placed in the right conditions. The naturally protected spore form allows Bacillus subtilis to be: (1) stable at room temperature (2) remain dormant to survive the harsh conditions of the stomach, and (3) germinate and proliferate when it reaches the intestine.
Bacillus clausii (recently renamed Alkalihalobacillus clausii) is a spore-forming probiotic. It is considered to be a soil-based organism (or SBO), because it can be found in the soil. Bacillus clausii has been found in the normal microflora of the gut in healthy adults and children. It is stable at room temperature (i.e., it does not require refrigeration), remains dormant to survive the harsh conditions of the stomach, and germinates and proliferates when it reaches the intestine.

If you do not love Qualia for any reason, simply get in touch via phone or email and let us know you’d like a refund. More on our 100-day guarantee here.
What do probiotics do?
+
Probiotics help support a healthy balance of beneficial bacteria in your gut. They promote digestive comfort, support nutrient absorption, and help maintain immune system function, since much of the immune system is housed in the gut. Certain strains may also support regularity and reduce occasional bloating or digestive upset. Formulas like Qualia Probiotic+ are designed with targeted, research-backed strains to support gut barrier integrity, microbial diversity, and whole-body wellness.\
What are the differences between probiotics and synbiotics?
+
Probiotics are live microorganisms that, when administered in adequate amounts, confer a health benefit on the host. Synbiotics are food ingredients or dietary supplements combining probiotics and prebiotics (compounds that support the growth or activity of beneficial microorganisms) with complementary actions.
How long does it take for probiotics to work?
+
Probiotics should start working immediately but you may not notice major results for a few days or even weeks. In the pre-market pilot study we conducted, most participants reported noticing benefits in one or more occasional digestive system complaints after seven days of taking Qualia Probiotic+.\
Do probiotics cause weight gain?
+
Probiotics have not been associated with weight gain. Probiotics are compounds that help support gut health and that, by doing so, may also support healthy metabolic function. Users also usually report noticing better digestion while taking probiotics.\
Is it OK to always take probiotics?
+
Qualia Probiotic+ is suitable for daily use. If you have health concerns or are taking medications, we advise you to consult your physician.\

A Comprehensive Approach
Eliminating senescent cells and promoting stem cell activity require dynamic strategies. That’s why Qualia Senolytic combines 9 ingredients that each target difference aspects of the senescent cell elimination process. And Qualia Stem Cell combines 15 ingredients to support complex stem cell needs such as proliferation, differentiation, and self-renewal.\
Impeccable Ingredient Sourcing
All ingredients in Qualia Senolytic and Qualia Stem Cell are ethically sourced and 3rd party tested. Additionally, the formulas include ingredients in patented and trademarked forms which are clinically studied, and ensure standardized potency and quality.\
Relationships Between Ingredients
Our science team pioneered the use of complex systems science in supplement formulations, which values the sum total effect of different ingredients in combination with each other, including deep study of their ideal dosage relationships, to achieve the greatest health benefits.\
Notable Ingredients In The Total Repair Bundle
QUERCEFIT® QUERCETIN PHYTOSOME
In Qualia Senolytic: Quercetin is a yellow plant pigment shown to support pruning of stressed cells and healthy changes to their behavior. But quercetin is poorly absorbed by the human body. Quercefit® is a unique and patented Phytosome® formulation of quercetin created to overcome this bioavailability obstacle. By creating a quercetin phytosome complex using sunflower lecithin, the bioavailability is dramatically enhanced.
FISETIN
In Qualia Senolytic: Fisetin is aflavonol found in certain fruits and vegetables, such as strawberriesand persimmon. Like quercetin, it was one of the first senolyticingredients ever discovered, and boasts one of the most impressivebodies of research of all senolytics. Fisetin has been stronglycorrelated with supporting body tissues by helping with pruning ofstressed cells.
LUTEOLIN
In Qualia Senolytic: Luteolin is a yellow plant pigmentused for centuries as a yellow dye. In fact, the latin name for yellowis luteus. Luteolin has a very similar molecularstructure to quercetin, and much like fisetin, luteolin has also shownevidence of senolytic properties by helping to manage senescent cellelimination.
LONGVIDA OPTIMIZED CURCUMIN® EXTRACT
Qualia Senolytic: Curcumin is the pigment in turmeric that results in itsyellow-orange coloring. Curcumin appears to have senolytic potential inintervertebral disc cells, but much like quercetin, it has poorbioavailability. LONGVIDA OPTIMIZED CURCUMIN® EXTRACT was developed to overcome this bioavailabilityhurdle and has been researched in human studies in areas includingcognitive function, mood, exercise recovery, and joint health.
CYANTHOX™ (sea buckthorn fruit extract)
In Qualia Stem Cell: CyanthOx™ is an extract from seabuckhorn fruit (native to Tibet) which has been clinically studied forsupporting the mobilization of stem cell types involved in the body’shealthy self-healing and self-renewal functions, including progenitorstem cells, endothelial stem cells, and lymphocytoid mesenchymal stemcells.\
ALOMAC® (Aloe macroclada extract)
In Qualia Stem Cell:Aloe macroclada is native to Madagascar, where it has been used by the Malagasy people for centuries to support natural healing and to promote rejuvenation and longevity. Alomac®, a standardized extract, has been studied for supporting healthy stem cell functions in humans, where it enhanced hematopoietic stem cells up 53%.\
OKINAWA MOZUKU EXTRACT
In Qualia Stem Cell: Mozukuis a brown seaweed grown in the coastal waters of Okinawa, where it is a foundational superfood in this blue zone’s diet. This extract contains ≥85% fucoidan, which provides support for a healthy immune system and for stem cell functions including stem cell mobilization and migration.\
AFANINPLUS® (Aphanizomenon flos-aquae extract)
In Qualia Stem Cell: AFAninPlus® is an extract derivedfrom Aphanizomenon flos-aquae (AFA), a blue-green algae harvested fromthe pristine, high-desert Klamath Lake in Southern Oregon. Clinically, AFA has been shown to boost levels of circulating hematopoietic stemcells by an average of 25%, to support a healthy immune system, and provide free radical protection, which are essential for the healthy activities and survival mechanisms of stem cells.\
Learn More About The Other Ingredients
+
Piperlongumine (from Piper Longum Root Extract) in Qualia Senolytic is an alkaloid found in many species of pepper plant, and found in abundance in the Asian long pepper plant (Piper longum), particularly in its root structure from which we source our piperlongumine. It is the only natural compound known to support a novel mechanism that helps manage senescent cells called oxidation resistance 1 or OXR1.\
Senactiv® in Qualia Senolytic is a patented blend of notoginseng root and chestnut rose hips. It has been shown to support exercise recovery and management of senescent cells in muscle. This provides an asset within our formula to help counteract the slow workout recoveries that are common with the human aging process.\
Olive Leaf Extract Oleuropein (a secoiridoid polyphenol) in Qualia Senolytic is found in high amounts within olive leaves. Our particular olive leaf extract is standardized for 40% oleuropein. A 2020 study showed that oleuropein supports the management of senescent chondrocytes—chondrocytes are the cells that create cartilage and support healthy joints (an area of our bodies which commonly experience noticeable negative aging symptoms).\
Milk Thistle Fruit Extract in Qualia Senolytic. The seed-like fruits from milk thistle (Silybum marianum) have been in Mediterranean diets for over 2,000 years. The main active substance in milk thistle is silymarin. Our extract is standardized to 80% silymarin by spectrophotometry, and has potential in playing a complementary role to other ingredients in Qualia Senolytic, particularly the stressed cell support of curcumin.\
Soybean Seed Extract in Qualia Senolytic. Soybean seeds, like most legumes, contain a type of bioflavonoid called isoflavonoids. Our soybean seed extract is standardized at 40% isoflavones which shows promise as having a complementary role in our overall formulation. Daidzein, for example (an isoflavonoid in our extract) is one of the top four compounds identified as a possible candidate for helping manage some types of senescent cells.\
Astragalus root extract in Qualia Stem Cell.Astragalus membranaceus, or Huang Qi, is a perennial herb native to northeast Asia, flourishing in dry, sandy soils and regions such as mountain thickets and vast grasslands. In Traditional Chinese Medicine it is one of the most important herbs for tonifying Qi and enhancing vitality, and is also renowned for healing and promoting healthy blood circulation. Preclinical studies suggest that Astragalus root aids healing by promoting healthy stem cell proliferation and differentiation.\
Salvia miltiorrhiza extract in Qualia Stem Cell, known as Danshen in Traditional Chinese Medicine, primarily grows in the rich, earthy soils of East Asia’s temperate and subtropical regions along hillsides, moist stream banks, and in cool, shaded forests. Traditionally, Danshen was believed to have an affinity for the heart and was used to promote heart health, blood circulation, and healing. Preclinically, Danshen has been shown to support the healthy differentiation and proliferation of various stem cell types, leading to improved tissue health like heart and liver.\
White peony root extract in Qualia Stem Cell, is a Traditional Chinese Medicine native to central and eastern Asia that grows in a variety of environments, such as glasslands, woodland edges, dry meadows and sunny slopes. Paeoniflorin, a primary bioactive compound extracted from the roots of white peony, is known to maintain a balanced activity of CDC42, which is a crucial mechanism for stem cell rejuvenation. Preclinical studies show that paeoniflorin supports healthy stem cell proliferation, differentiation, and self-renewal, driving tissue regeneration.\
Sodium copper chlorophyllin in Qualia Stem Cell, is derived from chlorophyll, the vibrant green pigment found in plants and algae that plays a crucial role in photosynthesis. In preclinical studies, this compound helps protect stem cells against oxidative stress and supports healthy stem cell proliferation and differentiation.\
L-Serine in Qualia Stem Cell, is an amino acid essential for protein synthesis and plays a vital role in stem cell function and maintenance. It is also required for nucleotide synthesis - the basic building blocks of DNA and RNA - which supports stem cell division, proliferation, and growth.\
NucleoPrime® Nucleotides Blend in Qualia Stem Cell, are the complex molecules that form DNA and RNA. DNA is sometimes referred to as the "blueprint for life", and RNA translates the information stored in DNA to make the proteins and express our genes. They are essential compounds during times of tissue self-renewal so act a bit like fuel for numerous stem cell functions.\
Wellmune® soluble yeast beta glucan in Qualia Stem Cell, supports the body’s natural defenses. Over a dozen clinical trials demonstrate the ability of Wellmune® to help maintain a healthy immune system and support overall energy and vitality. The soluble beta-glucans in Wellmune support healthy stem cell migration, renewal, and survival mechanisms.\
Bromelain inQualia Stem Cell, is a mixture of protein digesting enzymes derived primarily from the stem and fruit of the pineapple known for its use in promoting a healthy inflammatory response, in supporting tissue healing, and in supporting the production of extracellular matrix components, such as collagen. Preclinical studies demonstrate their ability to support creation of a favorable microenvironment for stem cell activities and tissue regeneration.\
